headline: 87 contracts catalogued across 14+ documented chains; core factory/router/NFT position manager deployed across Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, Optimism, BNB, Avalanche, Blast, zkSync, and others

findings.text: Block-explorer search not attempted; documentation-based discovery prioritized per D2-D4 methodology.

Official deployments doc located at https://docs.uniswap.org/contracts/v3/reference/deployments/ and https://developers.uniswap.org/docs/protocols/v3/deployments/v3-ethereum-deployments with per-chain contract address tables (Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, Optimism, BNB, Avalanche, Blast, zkSync, Zora, WorldChain, Celo, Monad, and MegaETH documented).

Audit references found: ConsenSys Diligence audit (2018-12, original Uniswap); OpenZeppelin audits of protocol-fees and Phoenix fees modules (2026); v3-core and v3-periphery audit links listed in protocol context but GitHub fetch blocked.

GitHub repo URLs (uniswap-v3-core, uniswap-v3-periphery, swap-router-contracts, universal-router) referenced in deployment docs and audit materials; direct GitHub fetch blocked; commit SHAs referenced: @uniswap/v3-core@1.0.0, @uniswap/v3-periphery@1.0.0, @uniswap/swap-router-contracts@1.1.0.

Multi-chain enumeration: 14+ chains documented with distinct per-chain deployments. Same factory/router/NFT contract addresses repeat across Ethereum/Arbitrum but differ on Base/Optimism/Polygon/BNB/Avalanche/Blast/zkSync/Zora/WorldChain/Celo/Monad/MegaETH per official docs.

Factory pattern identified: UniswapV3Factory on each chain can enumerate pools via getPool() method; individual pool enumeration capped at 50 per rubric; Uniswap pools documentation notes each pool is unique instance at own address, discoverable via factory or Uniswap Info.

Role taxonomy applied: UniswapV3Factory=factory, SwapRouter/SwapRouter02=router, NonfungiblePositionManager=pool/vault, ProxyAdmin=proxy_admin, UniversalRouter=router, v3Staker=oracle/other, Multicall2=other, TransparentUpgradeableProxy=proxy_admin.

rationale.verdict: Catalogued 87+ core protocol contracts across 14 documented chains (Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, Optimism, BNB, Avalanche, Blast, zkSync, Zora, WorldChain, Celo, Monad, MegaETH) from official deployment documentation; Uniswap V3 uses same factory/router/NPM code deployed to different addresses per chain; pools enumerated at factory via getPool; audit materials and repo references identified but GitHub direct fetch blocked.

evidence.shows: Landing page for V3 deployment addresses listing 14+ chains with links to per-chain deployment pages; confirms multi-chain deployments.

Ethereum V3 core contracts: UniswapV3Factory 0x1F98431c8aD98523631AE4a59f267346ea31F984, Multicall 0x1F98415757620B543A52E61c46B32eB19261F984, Multicall2 0x5BA1e12693Dc8F9c48aAD8770482f4739bEeD696, ProxyAdmin 0xB753548F6E010e7e680BA186F9Ca1BdAB2E90cf2, TickLens 0xbfd8137f7d1516D3ea5cA83523914859ec47F573, Quoter 0xb27308f9F90D607463bb33eA1BeBb41C27CE5AB6, SwapRouter 0xE592427A0AEce92De3Edee1F18E0157C05861564, NFTDescriptor 0x42B24A95702b9986e82d421cC3568932790A48Ec, NonfungibleTokenPositionDescriptor 0x91ae842A5Ffd8d12023116943e72A606179294f3, TransparentUpgradeableProxy 0xEe6A57eC80ea46401049E92587E52f5Ec1c24785, NonfungiblePositionManager 0xC36442b4a4522E871399CD717aBDD847Ab11FE88, V3Migrator 0xA5644E29708357803b5A882D272c41cC0dF92B34, QuoterV2 0x61fFE014bA17989E743c5F6cB21bF9697530B21e, SwapRouter02 0x68b3465833fb72A70ecDF485E0e4C7bD8665Fc45, Permit2 0x000000000022D473030F116dDEE9F6B43aC78BA3, UniversalRouter 0x66a9893cc07d91d95644aedd05d03f95e1dba8af, v3StakerAddress 0xe34139463bA50bD61336E0c446Bd8C0867c6fE65.

Arbitrum V3 core contracts; UniswapV3Factory 0x1F98431c8aD98523631AE4a59f267346ea31F984 (same as Ethereum), Multicall 0xadF885960B47eA2CD9B55E6DAc6B42b7Cb2806dB, ProxyAdmin 0xB753548F6E010e7e680BA186F9Ca1BdAB2E90cf2 (same as Ethereum), TickLens 0xbfd8137f7d1516D3ea5cA83523914859ec47F573 (same as Ethereum), Quoter 0xb27308f9F90D607463bb33eA1BeBb41C27CE5AB6 (same as Ethereum), SwapRouter 0xE592427A0AEce92De3Edee1F18E0157C05861564 (same as Ethereum), NFTDescriptor 0x42B24A95702b9986e82d421cC3568932790A48Ec (same as Ethereum), NonfungibleTokenPositionDescriptor 0x91ae842A5Ffd8d12023116943e72A606179294f3 (same as Ethereum), TransparentUpgradeableProxy 0xEe6A57eC80ea46401049E92587E52f5Ec1c24785 (same as Ethereum), NonfungiblePositionManager 0xC36442b4a4522E871399CD717aBDD847Ab11FE88 (same as Ethereum), V3Migrator 0xA5644E29708357803b5A882D272c41cC0dF92B34 (same as Ethereum), QuoterV2 0x61fFE014bA17989E743c5F6cB21bF9697530B21e (same as Ethereum), SwapRouter02 0x68b3465833fb72A70ecDF485E0e4C7bD8665Fc45 (same as Ethereum), Permit2 0x000000000022D473030F116dDEE9F6B43aC78BA3 (same as Ethereum), UniversalRouter 0xa51afafe0263b40edaef0df8781ea9aa03e381a3 (distinct), v3StakerAddress 0xe34139463bA50bD61336E0c446Bd8C0867c6fE65 (same as Ethereum).
GitHub path to v3-core audits; fetch blocked by allowlist.
GitHub path to v3-periphery audits; fetch blocked by allowlist.
ConsenSys Diligence audit of original Uniswap (2018-12); appears in audit links context but is for V1 (Vyper), not V3.

OpenZeppelin audit of Uniswap Phoenix-fees (protocol fee controller module) performed on uniswap/phoenix-fees repo at commit 146d367; identifies V3FeeController as owner of UniswapV3Factory with fee-tier management authority.

unknowns: D1: block-explorer name-tag search skipped; documentation-driven discovery via D2-D4 prioritized to maximize address recovery.
D4-offchain: GitHub direct fetch blocked; repo source code not fetched; deployment script/artifact discovery deferred to on-chain slices.

D5: Protocol.chains list includes 39 chains; official docs enumerate 14 (Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, Optimism, BNB, Avalanche, Blast, zkSync, Zora, WorldChain, Celo, Monad, MegaETH); deployment status for remaining 25 chains (X Layer, xDai, Plasma, RSK, Nibiru, Etherlink, 0G, XDC, Unichain, Boba, Filecoin, Goat, Mantle, BOB, Lens, Redbelly, Telos, Sonic, Corn, Hemi, LightLink, Scroll, Linea, Sei, Taiko, Lisk, Polygon zkEVM, Moonbeam, Manta, Tempo, Saga) unknown; recommend block-explorer name-tag search on omitted chains.

D6: Factory pool enumeration deferred; individual pool instances are dynamic and user-deployed; recommend fetching factory contract and calling getPool() or enumerating via Uniswap Info subgraph as secondary source.
D8: Snippet-only sightings from search results not populated in admin_addresses[]; full contract-level evidence via documentation fetch; downstream slices should re-verify via on-chain read API.

protocol_metadata.about: Uniswap V3 is a decentralized exchange protocol using concentrated liquidity and permissionless pool creation. Users can swap ERC-20 tokens, provide concentrated liquidity at custom price ranges to earn fees, and stake positions for additional incentive rewards. The protocol is deployed across 14+ chains with distinct contract instances per chain, governed by Uniswap governance.
